exemplar.title: To what extent has Tortilla Mexican Grillâs acquisition of Chilango on May 23, 2022, improved its financial performance and market position?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Tortilla Mexican Grillâs acquisition of Chilango on May 23, 2022, improved its financial performance and market position?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear and sound understanding of the key concept of change
Consistently frames analysis (Force-Field, BCG, ratios) around change
Strong, organisation-specific connection to Tortilla and acquisition data
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally drifts into pure description without linking back to change
Key concept lacks a concise operational definition
Some ratio discussions are presented without tying them to the concept of change
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands place this at band 4: the student shows a strong understanding and maintains a clear, organisation-specific focus on change throughout most of the IA. Integration is solid but not fully woven into every paragraph, and academic rigour (precise definition, textbook citation) is missing, preventing a top-band 5.

strengths: All referenced documents (annual reports, press releases, database entries) are relevant to the case
Some documents provide sufficiently detailed financial data
weaknesses: Six secondary sources exceed the 3â5 limit, which automatically places this in band 1
Depth and range across documents are uneven
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the listed documents are relevant and some contain depth, the count exceeds five, which by rubric forces a band 1 (1 mark) regardless of depth.

strengths: Effective selection of three appropriate tools (Force-Field, BCG, ratio analysis)
Each tool is applied to the case company, showing clear relevance to the research question
weaknesses: Application depth is uneven (generic Force-Field points, single-year BCG justification)
No explicit rationale for tool choice against the research question
Some tool diagrams lack labels and quantitative criteria
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Selection of tools is strong (band 4) but their application is only adequately relevant (band 3), placing the holistic performance at band 3 (3 marks).
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Links integration costs to margin trends and liquidity ratios
Uses a range of data (revenues, margins, current ratio) to address the question
Demonstrates more than mere description, moving into analysis
weaknesses: Analysis remains surface-level in parts with calculation errors
Limited critical evaluation of strategic impact
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work moves beyond description into âsome analysisâ of the research question (band 3), but lacks the consistency and depth needed for band 4 or 5.

strengths: Conclusions mirror key findings on market position, revenue, and profitability
Research question is explicitly restated and addressed
weaknesses: Numeric error in reported 2023 revenue undermines consistency
No actionable recommendations or strategic priorities
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions are generally consistent with the evidence and answer the question (band 3), but the misreported figure and lack of actionable next steps reduce this to band 2 (2 marks).

strengths: IA follows prescribed sections and includes a table of contents
Logical ordering of introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion
weaknesses: Sections occasionally end mid-sentence, interrupting flow
Some paragraphs are incomplete, disrupting coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall structure is present, but intermittent mid-sentence breaks indicate only âlimited structureâ (band 1).

strengths: All required elements are included (title page, headings, figures, references)
weaknesses: Multiple typos and inconsistent formatting (typo in ROCE label, OCR artifacts)
Figure labels and numbering are inconsistent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While all presentation elements appear, pervasive formatting and labeling errors limit professionalism, placing this at band 1.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows the prescribed structure (introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, references) and includes a table of contents.  Logical flow is maintained throughout, satisfying the requirements for âappropriate structureâ.

Annual and investor reports, press releases and an industry database are all directly relevant and contain financial detail.  However, depth is uneven; some data (e.g. revenue trend) are extracted but others are only mentioned.  The documents therefore reach ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the wider range and depth required for the top band.
Title page details, table of contents, section headings, charts/figures and reference list are all present.  Thus all required presentation elements are included.
Six distinct secondary sources are listed, which places the submission in the rubric category of "more than 5 documents."  This automatically falls into the 1-mark band for number of documents.

Each tool is applied to Tortilla, but depth is mixed.  The Force-Field lists generic points; the BCG allocations are justified by single-year uplifts; ratio analysis sometimes mis-states figures.  Application is generally relevant yet not consistently rigorous, matching the âadequately applied / relevance not always clearâ descriptor.

The student selects several pieces of data (revenues, margins, current assets/liabilities, site-level uplifts) and employs them in discussion.  Selection covers the basics but omits comparative benchmarks and some calculations contain errors, so usage is adequate rather than âsufficientâ or âeffectiveâ.

Analysis moves beyond descriptionâlinking integration costs to falling margins, liquidity to acquisition cash use, etc.âbut often remains surface level and sometimes includes inaccuracies.  This aligns with âsome analysis of the research questionâ rather than mostly effective or thorough integration.
The research question is explicitly restated and answered in the concluding section, fulfilling the descriptor for an explicit answer aligned with evidence.

Three appropriate strategic/financial tools (Force-Field, BCG Matrix, multiple ratio analysis) are selected and each is suited to the research question.  This represents an effective, purposeful choice that goes beyond adequacy.

The conclusion clearly states that the acquisition improved market position and revenue but strained profitability, directly mirroring evidence presented.  Assertions are consistent with the analysed data, meeting the top band for this strand.

All analytical tools, data and commentary are focused on Tortilla Mexican Grill and its recently acquired Chilango sites.  The discussion of site-level sales uplifts, specific integration costs and financial statements shows solid, organisation-specific analysis throughout, though some figures are imprecise (e.g. âÂ£365.4 millionâ).  The connection is therefore strong but not flawless enough for full integration.

The student explicitly chooses the key concept of change and repeatedly frames sections (Force-Field, BCG, ratio movements) around how the acquisition "changed" Tortillaâs performance.  References to change are accurate and show sound conceptual grasp, but the analysis is not woven through every paragraph; several ratio discussions drift into pure description without linking back to change.  Hence the work demonstrates a strong understanding with partial rather than full integration.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly articulated, specifying the acquisition date, target company, and dual focus on financial performance and market position, which provides strong focus for the analysis.

Key concept âChangeâ is identified early but lacks a concise operational definition to guide analysis. Consider defining layers of change (strategic, operational, cultural) to deepen concept integration.
The table of contents demonstrates a logical and comprehensive structure, clearly mapping sections and subsections to the research question, which supports ease of navigation.
The ROCE formula refers to âCapital Employeeâ instead of âEmployed.â Correct this typo to maintain professional presentation.
Background section effectively situates Tortilla and Chilango but relies heavily on descriptive detail. Link background facts more directly to the concept of change for sharper relevance.
The definition of change is presented but cites a generic guide without academic precision. Incorporate a business management textbook definition to strengthen conceptual rigour.
Methodology section lists tools and sources but does not justify their selection against the research question. Provide rationale for choosing Force-Field, BCG, and ratio analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The Force-Field Analysis figure lacks clear labels and relative force magnitudes, reducing its analytical clarity. Annotate driving vs restraining forces and weightings for depth.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The text describing the Force-Field Analysis provides general drivers and restrainers but lacks critical evaluation of their strategic impact on Tortillaâs change process.
The BCG Matrix introduction explains the tool but does not specify quadrant boundary criteria. Define market share and growth thresholds for consistent classification.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The BCG figure omits axis labels and scale markers, hindering interpretation of relative market share and growth. Add axis titles and numeric scales for clarity.
The section ends mid-sentence (âHowever, Tortilla needs toâ) which interrupts the analytical flow. Ensure each paragraph concludes with a complete recommendation.
The revenue growth narrative presents impressive percentages but lacks a comparative table. Consider summarizing key revenue changes in a table to improve clarity.
Figure numbering is inconsistent, with two captions labeled as Figure 3. Verify sequential numbering to maintain clear presentation.
The GPM formula is correctly displayed but could be strengthened by specifying units consistently (e.g., percentages) and explaining variable selection.
The gross profit margin table skips data for 2022, showing 2021, 2023, and 2024. Confirm that data rows match the analysis timeline for coherence.
The interpretation of margin trends links well to integration costs but would benefit from quantifying the cost impact to support the claim with specific numeric examples.
Stray OCR fragments (âyearâ) appear in the Current Ratio section. Review and clean up text to eliminate distracting artifacts.
The Current Ratio table clearly presents data but lacks benchmark context. Explain that a ratio below 1.0 may signal liquidity concerns to enhance analysis.
The Current Ratio formula text is clear; adding a brief example calculation would reinforce understanding of how figures translate into the ratio metric.
The ROCE table nests a calculation in parentheses, which can obscure clarity. Separate the formula, computation, and result into distinct columns or notes.
The conclusion misreports 2023 revenue as Â£365.4 million instead of Â£65.7 million. Correct this figure to ensure conclusions remain consistent with evidence.
The conclusion explicitly answers the research question and summaries key findings on market position and financial performance, demonstrating strong coherence.
The conclusion could be enhanced by outlining specific strategic priorities or KPIs for Tortilla post-acquisition, linking recommendations to actionable next steps.
References show variety but formatting is inconsistent. Standardize citation style (e.g., italics for titles, uniform date formats, complete URLs) to improve presentation.
All listed references are secondary. To enrich analysis, consider incorporating primary data (e.g., interviews with management, customer surveys) to deepen evaluation.
